2604.05483.title: Can We Trust a Black-box LLM? LLM Untrustworthy Boundary Detection via Bias-Diffusion and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
2604.05483.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown a high capability in answering questions on a diverse range of topics. However, these models sometimes produce biased, ideologized or incorrect responses, limiting their applications if there is no clear understanding of which topics their answers can be trusted. In this research, we introduce a novel algorithm, named as GMRL-BD, designed to identify the untrustworthy boundaries (in terms of topics) of a given LLM, with black-box access to the LLM and under specific query constraints. Based on a general Knowledge Graph (KG) derived from Wikipedia, our algorithm incorporates with multiple reinforcement learning agents to efficiently identify topics (some nodes in KG) where the LLM is likely to generate biased answers. Our experiments demonstrated the efficiency of our algorithm, which can detect the untrustworthy boundary with just limited queries to the LLM. Additionally, we have released a new dataset containing popular LLMs including Llama2, Vicuna, Falcon, Qwen2, Gemma2 and Yi-1.5, along with labels indicating the topics on which each LLM is likely to be biased.
2604.05483.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在回答各種主題的問題上展現了很高的能力。<lb/>然而，這些模型有時會產生偏見、意識形態化或不正確的回應，限制了它們的應用，特別是在對其答案可信度的主題沒有清晰理解的情況下。<lb/>在本研究中，我們介紹了一種新算法，名為GMRL-BD，旨在識別給定LLM的不可信邊界（在主題方面），該算法在黑箱訪問LLM的情況下並在特定查詢限制下運行。<lb/>基於從維基百科衍生的一般知識圖譜（KG），我們的算法結合了多個強化學習代理，以有效識別LLM可能生成偏見答案的主題（KG中的某些節點）。<lb/>我們的實驗證明了我們算法的效率，該算法僅需有限的查詢即可檢測不可信邊界。<lb/>此外，我們還發布了一個新數據集，包含流行的LLM，如Llama2、Vicuna、Falcon、Qwen2、Gemma2和Yi-1.5，以及標籤，指示每個LLM可能存在偏見的主題。
2604.05483.author: Xiaotian Zhou et.al.
2604.05483.authors: Xiaotian Zhou, Di Tang, Xiaofeng Wang, Xiaozhong Liu
